speaker: MALVOLIO
text: Were not you even now with the Countess

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: Olivia?

speaker: VIOLA
text: Even now, sir. On a moderate pace I have since

speaker: VIOLA
text: arrived but hither.

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: She returns this ring to you, sir. You might

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: have saved me my pains to have taken it away

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: yourself. She adds, moreover, that you should put

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: your lord into a desperate assurance she will none

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: of him. And one thing more, that you be never so

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: hardy to come again in his affairs unless it be to

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: report your lord’s taking of this. Receive it so.

speaker: VIOLA
text: She took the ring of me. I’ll none of it.

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: Come, sir, you peevishly threw it to her, and

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: her will is it should be so returned.

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: If it be worth stooping for, there it

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: lies in your eye; if not, be it his that finds it.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I left no ring with her. What means this lady?

speaker: VIOLA
text: Fortune forbid my outside have not charmed her!

speaker: VIOLA
text: She made good view of me, indeed so much

speaker: VIOLA
text: That methought her eyes had lost her tongue,

speaker: VIOLA
text: For she did speak in starts distractedly.

speaker: VIOLA
text: She loves me, sure! The cunning of her passion

speaker: VIOLA
text: Invites me in this churlish messenger.

speaker: VIOLA
text: None of my lord’s ring? Why, he sent her none!

speaker: VIOLA
text: I am the man. If it be so, as ’tis,

speaker: VIOLA
text: Poor lady, she were better love a dream.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Disguise, I see thou art a wickedness

speaker: VIOLA
text: Wherein the pregnant enemy does much.

speaker: VIOLA
text: How easy is it for the proper false

speaker: VIOLA
text: In women’s waxen hearts to set their forms!

speaker: VIOLA
text: Alas, our frailty is the cause, not we,

speaker: VIOLA
text: For such as we are made of, such we be.

speaker: VIOLA
text: How will this fadge? My master loves her dearly,

speaker: VIOLA
text: And I, poor monster, fond as much on him,

speaker: VIOLA
text: And she, mistaken, seems to dote on me.

speaker: VIOLA
text: What will become of this? As I am man,

speaker: VIOLA
text: My state is desperate for my master’s love.

speaker: VIOLA
text: As I am woman (now, alas the day!),

speaker: VIOLA
text: What thriftless sighs shall poor Olivia breathe!

speaker: VIOLA
text: O Time, thou must untangle this, not I.

speaker: VIOLA
text: It is too hard a knot for me t’ untie.
